It’s been 19 days since I last queued into Counter-Strike. My rig is warm, fans cycling. I’m hovering over Play. The time away has been costly. Reaction time slipped by milliseconds—small, but fatal. My hands twitch. Frame rate locked. Ping stable. Counter Strike does not negotiate. The match is found. I load in, tentative. Crosshair slightly off. An enemy peeks. The mouse moves almost without permission. A shot rings out. A hit. Endorphins surge. Counter-Strike has me again.

It’s been weeks since I last logged into Minecraft. The launcher finished updating. World loads in. Chunks generating. I stand still letting the ticks settle. Controls feel off. Timing slightly wrong. Muscle memory rusty. My base is quiet. Tools familiar but heavy. I step forward, cautious. A cave opens. Footsteps echo. A skeleton rattles somewhere unseen. Heart rate up. Aim steadies. Pick swings. First block breaks. The rhythm returns. Minecraft has me again.
